steps.title: Recognize the difference of squares inside the parentheses
steps.content: The expression inside the parentheses is $x^2 - 100y^2$. Notice that $x^2 = (x)^2$ and $100y^2 = (10y)^2$, so this fits the pattern $a^2 - b^2 = (a-b)(a+b)$ with $a = x$ and $b = 10y$.

steps.title: Factor the difference of squares
steps.content: $$x^2 - 100y^2 = (x - 10y)(x + 10y).$$ Multiplying back in the outer factor of 14: $$14(x^2 - 100y^2) = 14(x - 10y)(x + 10y).$$

steps.title: Evaluate each answer choice
steps.content:
• Choice A: $14(x - 10y)(x - 10y)$ has a middle term when expanded, so it is not the original.
  • Choice B: $14(x - 10y)(x + 10y)$ is exactly the difference-of-squares form.
  • Choice C: $14(x - 2y)(x - 50y)$ creates a middle term and the wrong sign on the $y^2$ term.
  • Choice D: $14(x - 2y)(x + 50y)$ also creates a middle term.
That leaves choice B.

steps.title: Answer
steps.content: $14(x-10y)(x+10y)$ matches choice B, so B is correct.
